items.placeName: Phở gánh Hà Nội ( Pho in Shoulder poles )

facebook.caption:
Under $15 for a bowl of pho in Phu Quoc. Real Hanoi-style. Served from shoulder poles the old way.

Phở gánh Hà Nội at 8B Hùng Vương keeps it traditional. 194 reviews, 4.2 stars. Locals eat here. Tourists walk straight past.

Broth that takes hours. Price that doesn't hurt. Skip the resort breakfast once.

Save this for your Phu Quoc trip.
facebook.video_caption: Watch the pho land on the table at Phở gánh Hà Nội. Broth poured fresh, herbs piled high, shoulder pole still swinging. Under $15. This is the real morning in Phu Quoc.

instagram.caption:

Squid noodle soup for under $15. In Phu Quoc. At a spot with 671 reviews.

Hủ Tiếu Mực Tư Hường on Đ. 30 Tháng 4 is what locals actually eat. Not the beachfront tourist traps. This. Fresh mực, rich broth, prices that make no sense for an island destination.

4.3 stars and still flying under most tourist radars. That gap won't last.

facebook.caption:
4.8 stars. 2,356 reviews. On a beach in Ông Lang that most tourists never reach.

Camia Restaurant sits right on the sand at Lê Thúc Nha Street. The crowd is small. The rating is not.

2,356 people rated this place. Almost all of them came back impressed. That kind of consistency is rare anywhere in Vietnam.

If you're staying near Ông Lang, this is the dinner. Save this for your Phu Quoc trip.
